join_order.build: pre(indexes/pre_adsh_tagver_set.bin — pre-sorted uint64_t array, 8,631,890 unique keys, 69MB)
join_order.probe: num(USD-2023 rows collected into ProbeEntry buffer, then sorted by key)
join_order.notes: Both the anti-join array (on disk, pre-sorted) and the probe buffer (sorted in memory before merge) are sorted ascending by the same uint64_t key = ((uint64_t)(uint32_t)adsh_code << 32) | (uint32_t)tagver_code. Single-pass merge: advance probe pointer and anti pointer together, O(n_anti + n_probes) = O(8.6M + 67K). Sequential scan of 69MB at ~50GB/s ≈ 1-2ms vs 1.5M random accesses in binary search approach. madvise(MADV_SEQUENTIAL) on the anti-join mmap so hardware prefetch kicks in immediately.

filters.notes: num is sorted (uom_code ASC, ddate ASC). mmap uom_code.bin, std::lower_bound + std::upper_bound to find exact [r_usd_start, r_usd_end). No row-level filter needed in the scan loop — row range is exact.

Within USD range, ddate is sorted ASC. mmap ddate.bin, std::lower_bound(20230101) + std::upper_bound(20231231) on [r_usd_start, r_usd_end) to find exact [r_2023_start, r_2023_end). Combined selectivity ~0.17% ≈ 67K rows. No ddate row-level filter needed.
std::isnan(v) check in the collect phase. Skip row if true.

scan.prefetch_strategy: After finding r_2023_start/r_2023_end via binary search, call madvise(MADV_WILLNEED) on the [r_2023_start, r_2023_end) byte ranges for adsh_code.bin, tagver_code.bin, value.bin. Byte range for row range [lo, hi) in column with element_size E: offset = lo * E, length = (hi - lo) * E. The uom_code and ddate mmaps are used only for binary search (top-level nodes fit in L3 after binary search) — no explicit madvise needed for them.

data_structures.probe_buffer: vector<ProbeEntry> where ProbeEntry = {uint64_t key; int32_t tagver_code; double value;}. Capacity reserved to r_2023_end - r_2023_start (≈67K). Size ≈ 67K × 20B = 1.3MB — fits in L2 cache. Filled in a single sequential pass over [r_2023_start, r_2023_end), skipping NaN values and invalid tagver_codes. Sorted by key before merge.
data_structures.anti_join: mmap indexes/pre_adsh_tagver_set.bin with MADV_SEQUENTIAL. Header: uint64_t n_unique (8 bytes). Data: n_unique × uint64_t sorted ascending. Probe via single-pass merge scan (advance anti pointer alongside sorted probe pointer). NO random access, NO binary search, NO build_joins pre-fault phase.
data_structures.aggregation: unordered_map<int32_t, pair<int32_t,double>> with reserve(512). Key: tagver_code. Updated for each anti-join survivor (rows not found in anti array). Expected ~a few thousand survivors, ~200 groups. Fits in L1/L2 cache.

parallelism.notes: Only ~67K rows to scan. Thread creation + join overhead for 64 threads (~5-10ms) dominates at this row count. The merge-join itself (sequential 69MB scan) is inherently single-pass. No merge of thread-local aggregation maps needed.

indexes.purpose: sort_merge_anti_join — header is uint64_t n_unique (8 bytes), followed by n_unique sorted uint64_t keys. mmap with MADV_SEQUENTIAL. Merge with sorted probe buffer in a single pass. Key construction: ((uint64_t)(uint32_t)adsh_code << 32) | (uint32_t)tagver_code. tagver_code=-1 casts to 0xFFFFFFFF (valid non-matching key).
dict_lookup — resolve USD to int8_t usd_code. Layout: uint8_t N; N x {int8_t code, uint8_t slen, char[slen]}. NEVER hardcode usd_code.

output.decode: After top-100 selection by cnt DESC: mmap tag/tag_offsets.bin (uint32_t[TAG_COUNT+1]), tag/tag_data.bin (char[]), tag/version_offsets.bin (uint32_t[TAG_COUNT+1]), tag/version_data.bin (char[]). tag_str = tag_data[tag_offsets[tc] .. tag_offsets[tc+1]). ver_str = version_data[version_offsets[tc] .. version_offsets[tc+1]).

optimization_notes: Root cause (Category B — algorithm change): main_scan consumed 76% of total time (54ms) because 64-thread parallel binary search performed 67K × 23 = 1.5M random accesses into the 69MB pre_adsh_tagver_set sorted array, which exceeds the 44MB LLC. Even with pre-faulting (5ms build_joins), the binary search bottom-levels miss L3 and hit DRAM at random offsets. Fix: replace random binary search with sort-merge anti-join. Collect all ~67K valid probe entries {uint64_t key, int32_t tagver_code, double value} (1.3MB total, L2-resident) in a single sequential pass; sort by key (~0.5ms, fully in-cache); then single-pass merge with the sorted anti array — a sequential 69MB read at memory bandwidth (~1-2ms with MADV_SEQUENTIAL and hardware prefetch). Also eliminate the build_joins pre-fault phase (5ms) since the sequential merge naturally triggers hardware prefetch on a hot page cache. Switch to single-threaded: 67K rows is trivial and thread overhead dominated at this scale. Expected: collect+sort+merge = ~3ms, total ~5-10ms.
